title: Tamale Pie
cScaleSummary: C1 because the only cholesterol contributors are the small amounts of Parmigiano-Reggiano, low-fat sharp cheddar, and plain Greek yogurt, which keep the cholesterol per serving in the 1 to 25 mg range when divided across 6 servings.
ldlActiveSummary: LDL-Active because the recipe is built around black beans, lentils, mushrooms, onions, celery, tomatoes, chiles, and corn, stays low in animal fat, and develops flavor through aromatics, spice, simmering, and structure rather than saturated fat.
intro:
Tamale pie is an early 20th-century American casserole inspired by Mexican tamales and chili con carne, created as a practical oven-baked alternative to labor-intensive, corn-husk-wrapped tamales. Popularized in Texas, California, and the Midwest, it became a staple of mid-century home cooking, layering chili-style filling with cornmeal into a single, family-style dish that reflects the adaptable, borderland roots of Tex-Mex comfort food.


This version follows that same spirit of adaptation, replacing the traditional beef-heavy filling with vegetables while keeping the cornmeal and chile backbone intact. It honors tamale pie’s history as practical, flexible comfort food.

items.item: (10 oz) Ro-Tel diced tomatoes with green chiles

stepGroups.title: Soak the Beans
items: Place ¾ cup dried black beans in a bowl and cover with at least 3 inches of cold water. Soak 8 to 12 hours at room temperature, or up to 24 hours refrigerated. Drain before cooking

stepGroups.title: Cook the Beans
items: Transfer the drained beans to a pot and cover with cold water by about 2 inches. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a gentle simmer. Simmer uncovered for 75 to 95 minutes, until fully tender with skins intact. Drain and reserve ½ cup of the cooking liquid. Spread the beans in a single layer in a sheet pan and let steam dry at least 5 minutes and up to 30 minutes.

stepGroups.title: Preheat the oven to 350°F on Convection Bake.
Make the Polenta
items: In a bowl, stir ¾ cup yellow cornmeal into 1 cup cold water to form a smooth slurry. In a saucepan, bring 2 cups cold water to a boil. Slowly pour the slurry into the boiling water while stirring. Add 1 tablespoon olive oil and ½ teaspoon salt. Reduce heat to low, cover, and cook 30 minutes, stirring every 5 minutes. The mixture should be soft and spoonable. If it becomes too thick, thin with 1 to 2 tablespoons hot water. Let rest in the pan 10 minutes before assembling; it may cool to room temperature.

stepGroups.title: Cook the Lentils
items: Rinse ¼ cup brown or green lentils under cold water. Transfer to a saucepan with 1¼ cups cold water. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a gentle simmer. Simmer 18 minutes, until tender and holding their shape. Drain immediately and let steam dry at least 5 minutes and up to 30 minutes before adding to the filling.

stepGroups.title: Preheat the Skillet
items: Place a 12 inch skillet over medium heat and allow it to preheat while you complete the mise en place.

stepGroups.title: Prepare the Mise en Place
items:
While the beans, lentils, and cornmeal cook, prepare the following:

8 ounces cremini mushrooms, 4 ounces finely minced and 4 ounces small chopped

2 small onions, chopped

1 celery stalk, finely chopped

4 garlic cloves, minced

2 tablespoons chili powder

1 tablespoon ground cumin

½ teaspoon smoked paprika

Open and drain the 4 ounce can of diced green chiles


Have these ingredients measured and ready before beginning the filling.

stepGroups.title: Prepare the Filling
items: Add 1 teaspoon olive oil to the preheated 12 inch skillet over medium heat, then add the mushrooms. Cook 8 to 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until they release their moisture and it cooks off. Continue cooking 2 to 3 minutes more, until lightly browned.
Add the onions and celery. Season with salt and black pepper. Cook 5 minutes, until softened.
Add the plant based sausage and cook 4 minutes, pressing with a spatula to break it into small crumbles as it browns.
Stir in the 4 cloves of minced garlic, 2 Tbsp chili powder, 1 Tbsp cumin, and ½ tsp smoked paprika and cook 45 seconds.
Reduce heat to medium-low. Add the cooked black beans and mash about one quarter of them directly in the pan. Add the cooked lentils.
Add 5 whole San Marzano tomatoes, hand crushed, with 2 tablespoons of their liquid.
Add the 4 ounce can diced green chiles and the entire 10 ounce bag of frozen corn.
Add 2 tablespoons of the reserved bean cooking liquid.
Simmer 6 minutes over medium-low heat. Taste and adjust salt if needed. Remove from heat.

stepGroups.title: Assemble the Casserole
items: Spoon about two thirds of the polenta into the dish. Spread it evenly across the bottom, then use the back of a spoon or an offset spatula to press and smooth the cornmeal up the sides to form a shallow lining.
Add the filling and spread it evenly inside the polenta shell.

In a small bowl, combine ½ cup fat-free refried beans, 2 Tbsp plain Greek yogurt, 1 Tbsp grated Parmigiano Reggiano, and 1 Tbsp warm water. Stir until smooth and spreadable. Spread this mixture evenly over the filling.
Spoon the remaining one third of the polenta over the top and gently spread to cover. Do not press down firmly.
Scatter ½ cup sliced black olives evenly over the surface.
In a small bowl, mix ½ cup low fat sharp cheddar, 1 Tbsp plain Greek yogurt, and 1 Tbsp grated Parmigiano Reggiano. Spread evenly over the top.

stepGroups.title: Bake
items: Bake at 350°F on Convection Bake for 45 minutes, until the filling is bubbling at the edges and the top is lightly browned.
Remove from the oven and let rest 10 minutes before serving.
